senses.definition: used to suggest that something is very certain or obvious, emphasizing the clear outcome or result of a situation

senses.definition: used to suggest that people can be unpredictable and diverse in their behavior, emphasizing the importance of being accepting and respectful of individual differences

examples.x: My neighbor's behavior is baffling to me, but I guess it's true what they say, there is nowt so queer as folk.

senses.definition: used to suggest that real-life events can be more surprising and unusual than anything that could be invented, emphasizing that reality is often unpredictable and extraordinary

examples.x: I thought the plot of that book was far-fetched until I learned that it was based on a true story; truth is stranger than fiction I suppose.

senses.definition: used to suggest that people who are seen as foolish or unimportant often have insights that others do not, because they have a unique perspective on the world or can notice what others cannot

examples.x: When the company's management team ignored the warnings of a janitor who had worked there for 20 years, they learned the hard way that jesters do oft prove prophets.

senses.definition: used to suggest that if someone does not expect too much from a situation or person, they will not be let down if things do not go as planned or hoped for

senses.definition: used to suggest that managing expectations and anticipating potential setbacks can help people avoid disappointment and maintain a more positive outlook
used to imply that the excitement or imagination of something can be more satisfying than the reality of it, suggesting that people should not build up their expectations too much
used to suggest that no matter how prepared someone is, there is always the possibility of unforeseen circumstances or events that can alter the outcome

senses.definition: used to suggest that people should not have unrealistic expectations or demand too much from something or someone that is limited in its abilities or capacities

senses.definition: used to suggest that future events can often be predicted by observing the signs or indications that precede them

examples.x: The sudden drop in temperature and change in wind direction were clear indications that a storm was coming, proving that coming events cast their shadows before.

senses.definition: used to suggest that even those in positions of power or influence may one day find themselves indebted to someone who is seemingly weaker or less significan

examples.x: The CEO of the company never forgot the young intern who had helped her get her start, proving that even a lion may come to be beholden to a mouse.

senses.definition: used to suggest that circumstances and situations can change unexpectedly, making it unwise to make absolute statements or predictions about the future

examples.x: He said he would never be able to run a marathon, but never is a long time - with dedication and training, he was able to complete a half marathon within a year.
